name: app.R
content:
# ============================================================ # PCA Step-by-Step Explainer - Shiny App # # Run with: shiny::runApp() # Requires: shiny, ggplot2 # ============================================================ library(shiny) library(ggplot2) # ---- Fixed dataset (reproducible) -------------------------- sample_correlated_data <- function(n, mu, Sigma) { z <- matrix(rnorm(n * length(mu)), ncol = length(mu)) x <- z %*% chol(Sigma) sweep(x, 2, mu, FUN = "+") } set.seed(42) n <- 40 Sigma <- matrix(c(4, 2.8, 2.8, 2.5), 2, 2) mu <- c(8, 6) raw <- sample_correlated_data(n, mu = mu, Sigma = Sigma) colnames(raw) <- c("Gene_A", "Gene_B") df_raw <- as.data.frame(raw) df_c <- data.frame( Gene_A = df_raw$Gene_A - mean(df_raw$Gene_A), Gene_B = df_raw$Gene_B - mean(df_raw$Gene_B) ) # PCA on centred data (no additional scaling) pca <- prcomp(df_c, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE) varexp <- round(pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100, 1) # PC1 angle in [0, 180] degrees pc1_ang <- atan2(pca$rotation[2, 1], pca$rotation[1, 1]) * 180 / pi if (pc1_ang < 0) pc1_ang <- pc1_ang + 180 pc2_ang <- pc1_ang + 90 # perpendicular; trig handles angles > 180 naturally # ---- Helper: project centred cloud onto a direction -------- project_onto <- function(angle_deg) { rad <- angle_deg * pi / 180 w <- c(cos(rad), sin(rad)) s <- as.numeric(as.matrix(df_c) %*% w) list( w = w, scores = s, variance = var(s), proj = data.frame( orig_x = df_c$Gene_A, orig_y = df_c$Gene_B, proj_x = s * w[1], proj_y = s * w[2], score = s ) ) } # ---- Shared ggplot theme ----------------------------------- pca_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + theme( panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"), axis.line = element_line(colour = "grey60"), plot.margin = margin(12, 12, 12, 12) ) # Axis limits (generous padding, same across all centred plots) xlim_c <- range(df_c$Gene_A) * 1.25 ylim_c <- range(df_c$Gene_B) * 1.25 # Arrow helper pc_arrow <- arrow(ends = "both", length = unit(0.11, "in"), type = "closed") # ---- Step metadata ----------------------------------------- STEPS <- 6 step_titles <- c( "Step 1 — The data", "Step 2 — Centre the data", "Step 3 — Find the direction of maximum variance", "Step 4 — PC1: the direction of maximum variance", "Step 5 — PC2: the orthogonal direction", "Step 6 — The new coordinate system" ) # ---- UI ---------------------------------------------------- ui <- fluidPage( tags$head(tags$style(HTML(" body { font-family: Georgia, serif; background: #f8f8f8; } h4.step-hdr { color: #8b1a1a; border-bottom: 2px solid #8b1a1a; padding-bottom: 5px; margin-bottom: 14px; font-size: 18px; } .expl { font-size: 14.5px; line-height: 1.75; color: #222; } .var-box { background: #fff5f5; border-left: 4px solid #8b1a1a; padding: 9px 14px; margin: 12px 0; font-size: 14px; } .btn-main { background: #8b1a1a; color: white; border: none; padding: 7px 22px; border-radius: 3px; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; } .btn-main:hover { background: #6b1010; color: white; } .btn-grey { background: #555; color: white; border: none; padding: 7px 22px; border-radius: 3px; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; } .btn-grey:hover { background: #333; color: white; } .progress-bar-wrap { height: 6px; background: #e0e0e0; border-radius: 3px; margin-bottom: 18px; } .progress-bar-fill { height: 6px; background: #8b1a1a; border-radius: 3px; transition: width 0.3s; } "))), br(), fluidRow( column(8, # Progress bar uiOutput("progress_bar"), h4(class = "step-hdr", textOutput("step_title")), plotOutput("main_plot", height = "430px"), br(), actionButton("btn_prev", "← Previous", class = "btn-grey"), actionButton("btn_next", "Next →", class = "btn-main", style = "margin-left: 10px;") ), column(4, br(), br(), br(), uiOutput("side_panel") ) ), br() ) # ---- Server ------------------------------------------------ server <- function(input, output, session) { step <- reactiveVal(1) observeEvent(input$btn_next, { if (step() < STEPS) step(step() + 1) }) observeEvent(input$btn_prev, { if (step() > 1) step(step() - 1) }) observeEvent(input$btn_find_pc1, { updateSliderInput(session, "angle", value = round(pc1_ang)) }) # ---- Progress bar ---------------------------------------- output$progress_bar <- renderUI({ pct <- round(step() / STEPS * 100) div(class = "progress-bar-wrap", div(class = "progress-bar-fill", style = paste0("width:", pct, "%;")) ) }) output$step_title <- renderText({ step_titles[step()] }) # ---- Side panel (explanation + controls) ----------------- output$side_panel <- renderUI({ s <- step() expl <- switch(s, # Step 1 p(class = "expl", "We have 40 samples, each measured for two genes.", "Each point is one sample, positioned by its Gene A and Gene B expression levels.", "The cloud has a clear elongated shape — the two genes are correlated." ), # Step 2 HTML('

We subtract the mean of each gene, moving the cloud so its centre lies at the origin (0, 0).

This step is mandatory in PCA — we measure spread relative to the mean, not relative to the measurement axes.

Grey points show the original positions; blue points show the centred data. The shape of the cloud is unchanged.

'), # Step 3 HTML('

PCA asks: in which direction does the cloud spread the most?

Use the slider to rotate the candidate direction. The red dots show where each sample projects onto the current axis. Watch how the spread — and the variance — of those projections changes.

'), # Step 4 HTML(paste0('

This is PC1 — the eigenvector of the covariance matrix corresponding to its largest eigenvalue.

It captures ', varexp[1], '% of the total variance.

Red projected points have negative scores (they fall behind the origin along PC1); blue projected points have positive scores. Both are correct — the PC axis runs in both directions from the origin.

')), # Step 5 HTML(paste0('

PC2 is the direction of maximum remaining variance, subject to one constraint: it must be perpendicular to PC1.

Orthogonality is not a geometric convenience — it ensures PC2 captures variation genuinely independent of PC1. The two components are uncorrelated by construction.

PC2 captures ', varexp[2], '% of the total variance.

')), # Step 6 HTML(paste0('

The data is now expressed in its new coordinate system. Each sample has a PC1 score and a PC2 score — its projections onto the two principal directions.

Together they retain ', varexp[1] + varexp[2], '% of the total variance. In a higher-dimensional dataset we keep only the first few PCs, discarding the directions that carry very little variance.

')) ) controls <- if (s == 3) { tagList( sliderInput("angle", "Direction angle (°):", min = 0, max = 180, value = 30, step = 1), div(class = "var-box", strong("Variance of projected scores: "), textOutput("var_display", inline = TRUE) ), br(), actionButton("btn_find_pc1", "Show direction of maximum variance →", class = "btn-main", style = "width:100%;") ) } else NULL tagList(expl, br(), controls) }) # ---- Variance readout (Step 3) --------------------------- output$var_display <- renderText({ req(step() == 3) ang <- if (is.null(input$angle)) 30 else input$angle sprintf("%.3f", project_onto(ang)$variance) }) # ---- Main plot ------------------------------------------- output$main_plot <- renderPlot({ s <- step() r <- 3.8 # arrow half-length # -- Step 1: raw data ------------------------------------ if (s == 1) { ggplot(df_raw, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B)) + geom_point(colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.85) + labs(x = "Gene A (expression)", y = "Gene B (expression)") + pca_theme # -- Step 2: centring ------------------------------------ } else if (s == 2) { ggplot() + geom_point(data = df_raw, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B), colour = "#bbbbbb", size = 3, alpha = 0.45) + geom_point(data = df_c, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B), colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.85) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.6) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.6) + annotate("point", x = 0, y = 0, shape = 3, size = 7, colour = "#8b1a1a", stroke = 2.5) + annotate("text", x = 0.15, y = min(df_c$Gene_B) * 1.1, label = "origin = mean", colour = "#8b1a1a", size = 4, hjust = 0, fontface = "italic") + labs(x = "Gene A (centred)", y = "Gene B (centred)") + pca_theme # -- Step 3: rotating direction -------------------------- } else if (s == 3) { # input$angle is created dynamically; use default 30° until slider exists ang <- if (is.null(input$angle)) 30 else input$angle res <- project_onto(ang) ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_segment(data = res$proj, aes(x = orig_x, y = orig_y, xend = proj_x, yend = proj_y), colour = "#cccccc", linewidth = 0.4) + geom_point(data = res$proj, aes(proj_x, proj_y), colour = "#8b1a1a", size = 2.2, alpha = 0.85) + geom_point(data = df_c, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B), colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.85) + annotate("segment", x = -r * res$w[1], y = -r * res$w[2], xend = r * res$w[1], yend = r * res$w[2], colour = "#e07b00", linewidth = 1.7, arrow = pc_arrow) + coord_fixed(xlim = xlim_c, ylim = ylim_c) + labs(x = "Gene A (centred)", y = "Gene B (centred)") + pca_theme # -- Step 4: PC1 with signed projections ----------------- } else if (s == 4) { res <- project_onto(pc1_ang) lx <- r * res$w[1]; ly <- r * res$w[2] lab_x <- if (lx > 0) lx * 0.82 else lx * 0.82 lab_y <- ly * 0.82 + 0.5 ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_segment(data = res$proj, aes(x = orig_x, y = orig_y, xend = proj_x, yend = proj_y), colour = "#cccccc", linewidth = 0.4) + geom_point(data = res$proj, aes(proj_x, proj_y, colour = score > 0), size = 2.8) + scale_colour_manual( values = c("TRUE" = "#2c7abf", "FALSE" = "#c0392b"), labels = c("TRUE" = "positive score", "FALSE" = "negative score"), name = "Projection:" ) + geom_point(data = df_c, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B), colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.35) + annotate("segment", x = -r * res$w[1], y = -r * res$w[2], xend = r * res$w[1], yend = r * res$w[2], colour = "#e07b00", linewidth = 2, arrow = pc_arrow) + annotate("label", x = lab_x, y = lab_y, label = paste0("PC1 (", varexp[1], "%)"), colour = "#e07b00", fill = "white", size = 4.5, fontface = "bold", label.size = 0) + coord_fixed(xlim = xlim_c, ylim = ylim_c) + labs(x = "Gene A (centred)", y = "Gene B (centred)") + pca_theme + theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.text = element_text(size = 11)) # -- Step 5: PC1 + PC2 ----------------------------------- } else if (s == 5) { r1 <- project_onto(pc1_ang) r2 <- project_onto(pc2_ang) # Label positions: offset away from each arrow tip lx1 <- r * r1$w[1] * 0.8; ly1 <- r * r1$w[2] * 0.8 + 0.5 lx2 <- r * r2$w[1] * 0.8 + 0.3; ly2 <- r * r2$w[2] * 0.8 ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_point(data = df_c, aes(Gene_A, Gene_B), colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + # PC1 annotate("segment", x = -r * r1$w[1], y = -r * r1$w[2], xend = r * r1$w[1], yend = r * r1$w[2], colour = "#e07b00", linewidth = 2, arrow = pc_arrow) + annotate("label", x = lx1, y = ly1, label = paste0("PC1 (", varexp[1], "%)"), colour = "#e07b00", fill = "white", size = 4.2, fontface = "bold", label.size = 0) + # PC2 annotate("segment", x = -r * r2$w[1], y = -r * r2$w[2], xend = r * r2$w[1], yend = r * r2$w[2], colour = "#8b1a1a", linewidth = 2, arrow = pc_arrow) + annotate("label", x = lx2, y = ly2, label = paste0("PC2 (", varexp[2], "%)"), colour = "#8b1a1a", fill = "white", size = 4.2, fontface = "bold", label.size = 0) + # Right-angle indicator at origin annotate("point", x = 0, y = 0, shape = 15, size = 4, colour = "grey45") + coord_fixed(xlim = xlim_c, ylim = ylim_c) + labs(x = "Gene A (centred)", y = "Gene B (centred)") + pca_theme # -- Step 6: scores plot --------------------------------- } else if (s == 6) { df_sc <- as.data.frame(pca$x) ggplot(df_sc, aes(PC1, PC2)) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.5) + geom_point(colour = "#2c5f8a", size = 3, alpha = 0.85) + labs( x = paste0("PC1 (", varexp[1], "% of variance)"), y = paste0("PC2 (", varexp[2], "% of variance)") ) + coord_fixed() + pca_theme } }) } shinyApp(ui, server)
